demographics.alt: Profile cards being filled in - getting to know your business context
demographics.ariaDescription: Three organized profile cards with checkmarks representing the process of understanding your business

pain.alt: Two disconnected systems with a broken link between them
pain.ariaDescription: Two separate system boxes with a broken connection showing business friction and disconnection

impact.alt: A declining bar chart with an alert badge and clock
impact.ariaDescription: A bar chart trending downward with an alert triangle and a clock indicating time-sensitive urgency

results.alt: A checklist document with completed items and a forward arrow
results.ariaDescription: A document with completed checklist items and a forward arrow indicating a clear action path ahead

sales.alt: A sales funnel with leads entering at top and a win at the bottom
sales.ariaDescription: A funnel showing leads entering at the top with a star representing a closed deal at the bottom

service.alt: Stacked support tickets with chat bubbles and a resolve checkmark
service.ariaDescription: A stack of support tickets with a resolution checkmark and chat bubbles indicating customer inquiries

ops.alt: A central workflow node connected to surrounding process boxes
ops.ariaDescription: A flowchart with a highlighted central gear node connected to input and output process boxes

marketing.alt: A megaphone broadcasting to a lead network with an upward trend bar chart
marketing.ariaDescription: A megaphone with sound waves reaching a network of leads, with a rising bar chart showing growth potential

questions.label: Which industry are you in?
questions.notes: Use existing exhaustive industry dropdown; do not redesign.

questions.label: Which sub-industry best fits your business?
questions.notes: Use existing exhaustive sub-industry dropdown; do not redesign.

questions.label: How many people work at your company?
What best describes your role?
options: Evaluating on behalf of someone else

condition.equals: Evaluating on behalf of someone else

questions.label: Where is your operation breaking down most?
How are you handling this today?
What do you think is really driving this?
How would you describe your challenge in your own words?
How is this problem showing up in your business?
options: General inefficiency - things take longer than they should

questions.label: How pressured do you feel to solve this?
options: Critical - it's affecting us right now
High - we need a solution within weeks
Medium - important but not on fire

Sales process: Spreadsheets and email - that's basically it
A CRM we're not really using well
Multiple tools that don't connect to each other
Internal ops & admin: Spreadsheets multiple people try to maintain
Meetings that could have been a process
Marketing & lead generation: Scattered across social, email, and ads with no unified view
Running campaigns but can't tell what's actually working
We know we need to do more but don't know where to start
A marketing tool we've outgrown or barely use

Sales process: No visibility into where things stand
Leads go cold because follow-up is inconsistent
Proposals take too long or get lost
We don't know what's working and what isn't
Customer service: No single place to see all customer issues
Too much depends on individual people
No process for escalation or follow-through
We can't tell what's resolved and what isn't
Internal ops & admin: Too many tools that don't connect
The way we work hasn't changed even though the team has
Marketing & lead generation: No way to track what's generating leads vs. wasting budget
Campaigns go out but there's no follow-through to sales
Content and messaging are inconsistent across channels
We're guessing instead of using data

PP4Placeholders.Sales process: Tell us what's happening - e.g., we lose track of leads after the first call and nobody owns follow-up...
PP4Placeholders.Customer service: Tell us what's happening - e.g., customers have to repeat themselves every time they contact us...
PP4Placeholders.Internal ops & admin: Tell us what's happening - e.g., approvals get stuck and nobody knows where things stand...
PP4Placeholders.Marketing & lead generation: Tell us what's happening - e.g., we spend money on ads but have no idea which ones actually turn into customers...

non_decision_maker_roles: Evaluating on behalf of someone else
outputFraming.rule: Decision makers get act-now framing. Others get bring-to-leadership framing.
